summary: Backend testing complete for SWR curve Q-factor fix. All 6 tests passed (100%). Verified that SWR curve V-shape correctly varies with element count and element diameter. 2-element (Q~8) produces wider/flatter curve than 5-element (Q~17). Thin elements (0.25") have higher Q than thick (0.75"). All endpoints working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Physics.py lines 1430-1481: antenna_q calculation now correctly applies element_count_q_mult (0.7 for 2-el, 1.4 for 5-el), spacing_q_mult, and matching_q_mult to produce varying Q based on antenna configuration
Physics.py lines 482-488: apply_matching_network uses dia_q_info.q_ratio * eq_mult to compute antenna_q_match dynamically
Physics.py lines 1644-1680: Smith chart sweep correctly uses smith_q from matching_info.antenna_q_used and cap_pf_used for consistent impedance transformation
Physics.py lines 266-322: compute_diameter_q_factor correctly calculates omega thickness parameter and derives q_ratio, bandwidth_mult, and swr_curve_exponent
seed_data_creation: None - tests use inline payload data
context_for_next_testing_agent: SWR curve Q-factor physics verified working. Key test data: 2-element Q=8.4 vs 5-element Q=16.8 (expected 2x difference). Thin elements (0.25") q_ratio=1.10 vs thick (0.75") q_ratio=0.94. SWR curve has 61 points spanning 0.6 MHz with proper V-shape varying by Q.

SWR curve center matches matched_swr.evidence: SWR curve has valid minimum with proper V/U shape based on Q factor

Thin vs thick element Q effect.evidence: Thin (0.25"): q_ratio=1.1038, antenna_q=13.25; Thick (0.75"): q_ratio=0.9393, antenna_q=11.27

swr_curve data validity.evidence: 61 points with frequency, swr, channel fields; SWR range 3.94-5.65 for sample config

/api/gamma-fine-tune endpoint.evidence: Returns optimized_swr=1.002, feedpoint_impedance=29.9, gamma_recipe with ideal_bar_position=7.56
